name: Terraform (IBM/HashiCorp)
description: Terraform (IBM/HashiCorp) — Infrastructure-as-code tool for provisioning cloud resources across AWS, Azure, GCP, and 300+ providers.

seo.title: Terraform (IBM/HashiCorp) — CI/CD for Applications
seo.meta_description: Terraform (IBM/HashiCorp): Terraform (IBM/HashiCorp) — Infrastructure-as-code tool for provisioning cloud resources across AWS, Azure, GCP, and 300+ providers.

when_to_use: Declarative IaC with plan/apply workflow and state
Module registry for reusable infrastructure components
Drift detection and remediation for cloud resources

pros: 300+ providers, covers nearly every cloud and SaaS
Skills transfer across companies and clouds
cons: BSL license move pushed community to OpenTofu
State management is still the main foot-gun
HCL gets ugly for complex logic
review.verdict: Still the default IaC tool, but evaluate OpenTofu seriously given the licensing change.
review.best_for: Multi-cloud teams needing mature IaC with a massive provider and module ecosystem.
review.not_for: Teams already committed to CDK, Pulumi, or fully cloud-native IaC.
